name: Instant Pot Duo 7-in-1 Electric Pressure Cooker, 6 Quart

specs.Cooking Functions: 7 — Pressure Cook, Slow Cook, Rice, Sauté, Steam, Keep Warm, Yogurt
specs.Included Accessories: Stainless steel steam rack, rice paddle, soup spoon, measuring cup

whatWorks: Pressure cooking cuts braised meats, beans, and soups to a fraction of stovetop time — pulled pork in 90 minutes, chickpeas from dry in 40 minutes. Owners who cook dried legumes regularly save hours per week.
Thirteen one-touch programs cover the most common tasks without needing to dial in custom pressure times — rice, soup, beans, and poultry all have dedicated presets that work well out of the box.
The online recipe community is enormous — millions of Instant Pot Duo recipes across YouTube, Reddit, and dedicated blogs mean almost any dish has a tested, crowd-verified recipe ready to follow.
The stainless steel inner pot is dishwasher safe, and the steam rack, paddle, and spoon all clean easily. The lid separates into a few components for thorough cleaning between cooks.
worthKnowing: The sealing ring absorbs strong food odors from curries, garlic, and chili. Subsequent cooks can carry a faint ghost of the previous meal. Many owners buy a second sealing ring to keep a dedicated dairy/sweet ring separate.

The slow cook function runs cooler than a dedicated slow cooker — most recipes developed for a standalone slow cooker will need 1–2 extra hours. Not a substitute if slow cooking is your main use case.

The manual is dense and not beginner-friendly on pressure cooking basics — liquid minimums, natural vs. quick release timing, and which foods to avoid are buried. Plan to spend 30 minutes watching tutorial videos before your first cook.
The stainless inner pot can discolour over time from mineral deposits and high-heat sautéing. It's purely cosmetic and doesn't affect performance, but some owners are bothered by it.

commonComplaints.title: Sealing ring smells like last night's…
commonComplaints.body: Sealing ring smells like last night's curry every time I open the lid. Made a yogurt after a chili cook and you could taste it. I ordered a spare.

commonComplaints.title: Slow cook doesn't get hot enough…
commonComplaints.body: Slow cook doesn't get hot enough — my pot roast was still underdone after the same time I'd use in my old Crock-Pot. Had to add two hours.

commonComplaints.title: The manual is confusing for a first-timer…
commonComplaints.body: The manual is confusing for a first-timer. How much liquid? What's natural release vs. quick? I had to watch YouTube videos before I felt comfortable using it.

commonComplaints.title: Inner pot discoloured after a few months…
commonComplaints.body: Inner pot turned bronze-ish after a few months of sautéing. I know it's just cosmetic but for a $130 appliance it shouldn't look like that.

verdict: The Instant Pot Duo is the best-selling multi-cooker in Canada for a reason: it replaces pressure cooker, slow cooker, rice cooker, steamer, and sauté pan in one appliance at a price that makes the trade-offs easy to accept. The sealing ring odor issue and slow cook temperature quirks are real and consistent across owners, but neither is a dealbreaker for the core pressure cooking use case. Buyers who meal prep, cook dried beans, or want quick weeknight braises will get more value per dollar from this than almost any other kitchen appliance under $150 CAD.

realBuyers.quote: I made beef stew in under an hour — tender meat, rich broth, everything cooked perfectly. My old stovetop method took four hours.
I set it before work and dinner is ready when I get home. The keep-warm function holds it perfectly for two hours.
I use it for rice, soups, beans, chicken — it's the most-used appliance in my kitchen by far. Replaced three separate gadgets.
The recipe community online is what sold me. There are millions of tested recipes and every question has already been asked and answered.

sources.title: Instant Pot Duo 7-in-1 review — Serious Eats

metaDescription: Instant Pot Duo 7-in-1 6Qt review for Canada: pressure cook, slow cook, rice, sauté in one pot. Honest on sealing ring odors and slow cook limitations. ~$129 CAD.
introText: The Instant Pot Duo 7-in-1 is the original multi-cooker that launched a category, combining pressure cooker, slow cooker, rice cooker, steamer, sauté pan, yogurt maker, and warmer in a single 6-quart pot. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B00FLYWNYQ) at around $129 CAD.
